## Changes
- Add the command **Migrate legacy progress records** to batch-convert old frontmatter checkIns into Markdown progress records.
- Add a regression test confirming KR reordering preserves related check-in history.
- Keep the existing lazy migration behavior for normal plugin writes.

## Verification
- 22/22 tests passed
- TypeScript check passed
- ESLint passed
- Production build passed

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/jingmengzhiyue/obsidian-okr-manager/compare/1.2.0...1.2.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Changes
- Store check-in history as readable Markdown progress records in each Objective file.
- Keep frontmatter focused on current OKR and KR state.
- Read legacy frontmatter checkIns arrays and migrate them to the Markdown progress section on the next plugin write.
- Update README files and plugin metadata for 1.2.0.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/jingmengzhiyue/obsidian-okr-manager/compare/1.1.0...1.2.0

releases.author: jingmengzhiyue
releases.description:
## Vault OKR Manager 1.1.0

### Fixes
- Fixed progress check-ins for duplicate key result IDs across different periods by carrying the selected period through dashboard, inline markdown actions, and the check-in modal.
- Preserved the exact current value entered during check-in instead of rounding it after deriving it back from progress percent.
- Added regression coverage for duplicate KR targeting and decimal current values.

### Development and release quality
- Declared Node.js 20+ as the required development runtime.
- Updated CI to run the test suite in addition to build and lint.
- Aligned README badges, package metadata, manifest, lockfile, and versions mapping for 1.1.0.

### Compatibility
- Minimum Obsidian version: 1.7.2.
- Desktop and mobile supported; this release is not desktop-only.

### Verification
- npm run build
- npm test
- npm run lint

releases.author: jingmengzhiyue
releases.description:
# 1.0.2 - Objective Editing & Progress Visualization Improvements

  ## Added

  - **Key Result management in Edit Objective modal** — You can now view, edit,
  and delete associated Key Results directly from the Objective edit dialog
  without returning to the dashboard.

  ## Changed

  - **Circular progress rings for Key Results** — Replaced linear progress bars
  with SVG ring indicators in the dashboard KR list. Progress states are
  color-coded: green (≥80%), yellow (≥40%), and red (<40%).
  - **Smoother Objective progress animation** — Progress fills now animate via CSS
  `transform: scaleX()` instead of `width`, improving rendering performance and
  visual smoothness.
  - **Faster expand/collapse interactions** — Objective card collapse/expand
  toggles now manipulate the DOM directly instead of triggering a full re-render.
  - **Improved layout containment** — Added `contain: layout` to the summary
  header, Objective cards, and KR rows to reduce reflow overhead.
  - **Cleaner KR rows** — Removed the inline current/target value display and
  per-row edit/delete buttons from dashboard KR rows; KR editing and deletion are
  now handled through the Objective edit modal.

  ## Compatibility

  - Minimum Obsidian version remains `1.7.2`.
  - Version mapping added to `versions.json` for `1.0.2`.
releases.name: 1.0.2 - Objective Editing & Progress Visualization Improvements

releases.author: jingmengzhiyue
releases.description:
**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/jingmengzhiyue/obsidian-okr-manager/compare/1.0.0...1.0.1


 ## What's Changed

  🔧 Fixed

  - Raised minAppVersion from 1.4.4 to 1.7.2 to match the Obsidian APIs actually
  used by the plugin:
    - Workspace.revealLeaf requires Obsidian 1.7.2
    - FileManager.trashFile requires Obsidian 1.6.6

  This ensures the plugin's declared minimum Obsidian version accurately reflects
  its runtime requirements and resolves obsidianmd/no-unsupported-api lint
  warnings.

  ⚠️ Compatibility Note

  Users on Obsidian versions older than 1.7.2 will need to upgrade Obsidian before
  installing this release.

  ## Full Changelog

  598ffb3...1.0.1
releases.name: 1.0.1 update  minAppVersion

releases.author: jingmengzhiyue
releases.description:
1.0.0 – Improve Responsiveness and Optimize OKR File Discovery

Latest

Overview  
This release improves the responsiveness of Vault OKR Manager and optimizes how OKR files are discovered and refreshed inside the vault.

New Features

Improved Inline Refresh  
Inline actions now update the current OKR view more reliably, reducing cases where changes are saved but not immediately reflected in the interface.

Scoped OKR File Discovery  
The plugin no longer relies on broader vault-wide file discovery for OKR loading. File lookup is now scoped more directly to the configured OKR directory structure for better performance.

Improvements  
Improved refresh behavior after inline OKR actions.  
Reduced redundant dashboard refreshes triggered by overlapping update paths.  
Optimized objective file discovery to avoid unnecessary vault-wide scanning.  
Improved overall responsiveness and consistency when managing OKRs.

Upgrade Notes  
This release focuses on performance and UI responsiveness improvements. Existing OKR functionality remains available.

If you are upgrading from an earlier version, no manual migration is required for these changes. All plugin data remains local to your Obsidian vault.

Compatibility  
Plugin ID remains unchanged: vault-okr-manager  
This release is compatible with existing Obsidian vaults.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/jingmengzhiyue/obsidian-okr-manager/compare/0.1.5...1.0.0
releases.name: 1.0.0 – Improve Responsiveness and Optimize OKR File Discovery

releases.author: jingmengzhiyue
releases.description:
## Overview

This release adds multilingual support and simplifies the plugin’s data structure by removing the dedicated check-in folder.

## New Features

### Multilingual Support

Vault OKR Manager now includes multilingual support, making the plugin easier to use across different language environments.

### Simplified Check-in Storage

The previous dedicated check-in folder structure has been removed to simplify file organization and reduce unnecessary folder creation inside the vault.

## Improvements

- Added support for multiple interface languages.
- Simplified the plugin’s vault storage structure.
- Removed the legacy check-in folder workflow.
- Improved overall maintainability and compatibility with cleaner data handling.

## Upgrade Notes

This release updates how check-in related data is organized. Existing OKR functionality remains available.

If you previously used the check-in folder, please verify your existing data after upgrading. No external services are used, and all plugin data remains local to your Obsidian vault.

## Compatibility

- Plugin ID remains unchanged: `vault-okr-manager`
- This release is compatible with existing Obsidian vaults.
releases.name: 0.1.5 – Add Multilingual Support and Simplify Check-in Storage

releases.author: jingmengzhiyue
releases.description:
## Release Notes

### Overview

This release introduces several usability and workflow improvements to the Obsidian OKR plugin, focusing on a more modern interaction experience, better deadline management, and automated version maintenance.

### New Features

#### Drag-and-Drop Reordering for Key Results

The previous button-based move up / move down interaction has been replaced or improved with a more intuitive reordering experience.

Users can now reorder Keys or Key Results more naturally through drag-and-drop or an equivalent modern interaction pattern. The updated order is persisted correctly and remains compatible with existing OKR data.

#### Overdue Deadline Reminder

OKRs with configured deadlines now support overdue detection.

When an OKR passes its deadline, the plugin clearly marks it as overdue and provides a visible reminder to help users identify items that require attention.

#### Deadline Extension Support

Users can now extend or postpone the deadline of an overdue OKR directly from the plugin interface.

After the deadline is updated, the overdue status is automatically recalculated. If the new deadline is in the future, the OKR will no longer be marked as overdue.

### Improvements

- Improved the overall interaction experience for managing OKR items.
- Added clearer visual feedback for overdue OKRs.
- Ensured deadline updates are persisted correctly.
- Maintained compatibility with existing OKR data structures.
- Updated the plugin version number automatically after implementation.

### Maintenance

- Updated relevant version references, including plugin manifest and package metadata where applicable.
- Verified that the plugin builds successfully after the changes.
- Preserved existing functionality while improving usability and maintainability.

### Upgrade Notes

This release should be backward compatible with existing OKR data. No manual migration is required.
releases.name: Improve OKR Reordering UX and Deadline Management

releases.author: jingmengzhiyue
releases.description:
This release addresses critical bugs related to progress tracking and sorting, while introducing a significant architectural change to how data is stored. Please note that this update contains breaking changes. We have streamlined the file structure to prevent directory clutter and improve data integrity.
⚠️ Breaking Changes
Deprecation of check-ins Directory: The separate check-ins directory has been deprecated and removed.
New Storage Strategy: Key Result progress updates are now consolidated directly into the main OKR configuration file. This prevents "directory explosion" caused by frequent updates and ensures all data remains in a single, manageable location.
Migration Required: If you are upgrading from a previous version, please back up your existing data. The old check-ins format is no longer supported, and the application will now read/write exclusively from the unified OKR config file.
🐛 Bug Fixes
Fixed Daily Update Limit: Resolved an issue where Key Result progress could only be updated once per day. You can now update your progress as many times as needed within a single day.
Fixed Sorting Issue: Addressed a bug that prevented Key Results from being sorted correctly. Sorting functionality is now fully operational and responsive.
💡 Why This Change?
The previous method of storing each check-in as a separate file led to excessive file system overhead and clutter (directory explosion), especially for active users. By merging progress data into the main OKR configuration, we achieve:
Cleaner File Structure: No more thousands of small files in subdirectories.
Better Performance: Reduced I/O operations when loading or saving OKR data.
Simplified Backup: Easier to manage and version control a single configuration file per OKR.
🙏 Acknowledgments
Thank you for your patience and feedback as we refine the core architecture of this project. We believe this change will provide a more stable and scalable foundation for future features.
releases.name: Release v0.1.2: Critical Fixes, Structural Refactor & Breaking Changes

releases.author: jingmengzhiyue
releases.description:
We are excited to announce the release of version 0.1.1! This update focuses on improving user flexibility and overall system responsiveness. We’ve listened to your feedback regarding data management and have introduced key improvements to how Key Results are handled, along with significant optimizations to plugin performance.
🚀 What’s New
1. Flexible Key Result Editing
Secondary Editing Support: You can now edit Key Results after they have been initially saved. This change provides greater flexibility, allowing you to refine your goals and metrics without needing to delete and recreate entries.
Updated Storage Mechanism: We have refactored the underlying storage logic for Key Results to support this new editability, ensuring data integrity while offering a smoother user experience.
2. Performance Optimizations
Smoother Plugin Experience: We’ve optimized the core rendering and interaction logic of the plugins. Users should notice reduced latency and a more fluid interface, especially when handling multiple tasks or switching between views.
🛠️ Technical Details
Refactored the KeyResult persistence layer to support update operations.
Improved event handling and UI thread management to boost plugin responsiveness.
🙏 Acknowledgments
Thank you to all our contributors and users for your valuable feedback and support. Your insights help us make this project better with every release!
releases.name: Release v0.1.1: Enhanced Key Result Management & Performance Improvements

releases.author: jingmengzhiyue
releases.description:
## 0.1.0

Initial public release of OKR Manager for Obsidian.

### Highlights
- Manage objectives and key results directly inside your vault
- Store all key results inside a single objective file
- Track progress with built-in check-ins
- Review OKRs from a dedicated dashboard
- Support week, month, quarter, and year periods

### Release assets
Download and install these files manually if needed:
- `main.js`
- `manifest.json`
- `styles.css`

Minimum Obsidian version: 1.4.0
License: MIT
[main.js](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27566003/main.js)
[manifest.json](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27566004/manifest.json)
[styles.css](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27566005/styles.css)
